rulings.Case Title: WHITNEY R LEEMAN PH.D. VS. MCCORMICK & COMPANY, INCORPORATED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel [Discovery]
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, August 26, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT MCCORMICK & COMPANY, INCORPORATED Motion To Compel [Discovery]. (For Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only - Part 1 of 2). Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is GRANTED. All objections based upon the "official information privilege," "full and complete in and of themselves," and "compound" are overruled. Plaintiff failed to argue the validity of these objections in the Opposition. Topic 1: SI2: All objections overruled. The Motion is granted. Plaintiff shall provide a further complete and straightforward response that omits her reference to her document production. RFP1: The overbroad and attorney-client privilege objections are sustained. All other objections are overruled. The Motion is denied as overbroad. RFP4: The attorney-client privilege and attorney work product objections are sustained. All other objections are overruled. The Motion is granted, plaintiff shall provide code-compliant responses. RFP9: The overbroad, attorney-client privilege, and attorney work product objections are sustained. All other objections are overruled. The Motion is denied as overbroad. Topic 2: SI4: All objections overruled. The Motion is granted. Plaintiff shall provide a complete and straightforward response. SI10: All objections overruled. The Motion is granted. Plaintiff shall provide a complete and straightforward response. RFP5: The attorney-client privilege and attorney work product objections are sustained. All other objections are overruled. The Motion is granted, plaintiff shall provide code-compliant responses. RFP7: The attorney-client privilege and attorney work product objections are sustained. All other objections are overruled. The Motion is granted, plaintiff shall provide code-compliant responses. SI7: All objections overruled. The Motion is granted. Plaintiff shall provide a complete and straightforward response. SI8: All objections overruled. The Motion is granted. Plaintiff shall provide a complete and straightforward response. SI9: All objections overruled. The Motion is granted. Plaintiff shall provide a complete and straightforward response. RFA6: All objections overruled. The Motion is granted. Plaintiff shall provide a code-compliant response. RFA7: All objections overruled. The Motion is granted. Plaintiff shall provide a code-compliant response. SI15: All objections overruled. The Motion is granted. Plaintiff shall provide a complete and straightforward response. (Continued to Part 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DENIS LE MOULLAC et al VS. DAYLIGHT FOODS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoenas For Private And Protected Records And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendants And Their Attorneys
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, August 26, 2014, Line 2, PLAINTIFF DENIS LE MOULLAC, JESSIE JEWITT Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoenas For Private And Protected Records And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendants And Their Attorneys. (For Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only - Part 1 of 2). Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiffs' Motion to Quash Subpoenas is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Betty and Alfred Delorenzi on behalf of The Delorenzi Family Trust: The Subpoena is quashed, in its entirety. Defendants' argument that "Defendants seek to understand the decedent's expenses," is insufficient to show how documents responsive to this Subpoena would be reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. Voce Communications, Inc.: The Subpoena is quashed as to "application for employment," "a description of her duties," and "all documents regarding services performed by Ms. Le Moullac for or on behalf of her employer" as over broad. Documents responsive to this Subpoena would not be reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. The Subpoena is modified, as follows: Request 3) for "Any and all written company policies¿" is limited to the policies in place at the time of Ms. Le Moullac's death. USC Academic Records: The Subpoena is quashed as to "college application," "any and all financial aid documents," "record of all tuition payments," "disciplinary notices and/or reports." Defendants' arguments that the records will be used "as predictor for future earning potential" and to "confirm that she was a college graduate" is insufficient to show how documents in the categories above would be reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. The parties are ordered to meet and confer before the hearing regarding a stipulation that plaintiffs will not seek damages for future support. In the Reply, plaintiffs argue that the claim for future support was "not pled and does not exist." In her declaration, plaintiffs' counsel declares, "future support was never pled." (Reply Decl., par. 6). However, in a July 28, 2014 letter, plaintiffs' counsel states that "while Plaintiffs originally raised future support in their respective discovery responses¿that is no longer the case." (Lenert Decl., Ex. G). In another correspondence, plaintiffs' counsel wrote, "As Plaintiffs are no longer making a claim for future support. . ." (Lenert Decl., Ex. E). The request for sanctions is DENIED. Plaintiffs' counsel is reminded to comply with CRC Rule 3.1110(f). (Continued to Part 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SEAN NICHOLSON et al VS. HOUSE OF AIR, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Quash Of Modify Depo Subpoena
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, August 26, 2014, Line 3, PLAINTIFF SEAN NICHOLSON, SHAWN NICHOLSON Motion To Quash Of Modify Deposition Subpoena. Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion to Quash Defendant HOA's July 1, 2014 Deposition Subpoena For Production of Business Records to Anthem Blue Cross is GRANTED. The Subpoena is overbroad. The Subpoena shall be limited to exclude Plaintiff Sean Nicholson's insurance application, policy quotations, premium determinations and history, and payment information relate to Sean Nicholson's payment of his premiums. The parties are ordered to meet and confer on the appropriate time range for the subpoena. The parties are ordered to meet and confer on the scope of the documents requested (ie documents related to treatment to cervical spine and shoulders). If the parties cannot agree to a limitation on time and scope, each party shall prepare a proposed order and bring the order to the hearing. The Court finds that Defendant HOA's counsel failed to properly meet and confer. Sanctions in the amount of $750 are awarded against Defendant House of Air, LLC and their attorney Dan Crowley and Associates for failing to meet and confer. Sanctions are payable within 2 weeks of notice of entry of this order. Counsel for HOA is reminded to comply with CRC Rule 3.1110(f). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcc@manningllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PETER CASTRO VS. OLD REPUBLIC TITLE COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, August 26, 2014, Line 13, DEFENDANT OLD REPUBLIC TITLE COMPANY's Demurrer To First Amended Complaint. The court overrules the demurrer in its entirety. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges the assignment of Ms. Grepo's claims at paragraphs 18-20 of the amended complaint. In Heritage Pacific Financial, LLC v. Monroy (2013) 215 Cal.App.4th 972, the allegations of the complaint were inconsistent with the promissory note, which was attached to the pleading. The Heritage court explained at 215 Cal.App.4th at 990: "We agree that the allegations in Heritage's SAC and the attached indorsement showed an assignment of Monroy's promissory note. However, the assignment of this contract right did not carry with it a transfer of WMC's tort rights. While no particular form of assignment is required, it is essential to the assignment of a right that the assignor manifests an intention to transfer 'the right.'" Here, the amended complaint has no such inconsistency and clearly states that plaintiff has the right to maintain the causes of action. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges the breach of contract cause of action. Paragraph 58 of the amended complaint alleges the written contract (escrow instructions, closing statement, and e-mails) required defendant to pay off plaintiff's loan and paragraph 59 alleges that defendant failed to do so. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges the contract terms according to their legal effect, i.e., plaintiff identifies the substance of the key terms that were breached. The fraud claims are sufficiently pleaded. Paragraphs 24-25 of the amended complaint allege details regarding who made the misrepresentations to Ms. Grepo regarding Saratoga Bancorp's authority to handle the payoff. Defendant complains that plaintiff does not plead more facts about defendant's fraudulent intent. A plaintiff need not particularize matters presumptively within the knowledge of the demurring defendant, however. See Turner v. Milstein (1951) 103 Cal.App.2d 651, 658. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
